title: The Soul Unto Itself
content:
The soul unto itself
    Is an imperial friend, --
    Or the most agonizing spy
    An enemy could send.

    Secure against its own,
    No treason it can fear;
    Itself its sovereign, of itself
    The soul should stand in awe.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "The Soul Unto Itself", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
